title: Financial administrative occupations n.e.c.
description: Analyze and coordinate the ongoing logistical functions of a firm or organization. Responsible for the entire life cycle of a product, including acquisition, distribution, internal allocation, delivery, and final disposal of resources. Design or analyze operational solutions for projects such as transportation optimization, network modeling, process and methods analysis, cost containment, capacity enhancement, routing and shipment optimization, or information management. Analyze product delivery or supply chain processes to identify or recommend changes. May manage route activity including invoicing, electronic bills, and shipment tracing. (Based on 33 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Monitor patrons for signs of compulsive gambling, offering assistance if necessary; answer questions about game rules and casino policies, and provide information on procedures or policies.

Supervise staff and monitor gambling tables to ensure security of the game; supervise the distribution of complimentary meals, hotel rooms, discounts, or other items given to players, based on length of play and amount bet; train new dealers.

Maintain records of transactions related to bets, payments, and customer interactions; record and summarize monetary exchanges, verify accuracy, and reconcile discrepancies; prepare itemized statements, bills, or invoices and record amounts due for items purchased or services rendered.

Perform cash handling tasks including exchanging currency for customers, processing payments, issuing receipts, and cashing checks; monitor checkout stations to ensure they have adequate cash available and are staffed appropriately.
Work as part of a team to ensure smooth operation of gaming activities, ensuring that house rules are followed and that patrons receive prompt and courteous service.
Provide assistance in the training and orientation of new cashiers and participate in maintaining a secure and regulated gaming environment.

Keep records of invoices and support documents, including recording specifics of malfunctioning machines needing repair; compile reports of cost factors, such as labor, production, storage, and equipment.
Analyze product delivery or supply chain processes and recommend changes, including monitoring gaming equipment functionality and taking action to resolve issues promptly.

Conduct monitoring and evaluation of staff performance; prepare written performance evaluations; establish or identify prices of goods, services, or admission, and tabulate bills, using calculators or cash registers.
Answer inquiries regarding rates, routing, or procedures; review operational expenses, budget estimates, and collection reports for accuracy; calculate financial fees, interest, and service charges.
Perform general administrative tasks, such as answering telephones, scheduling appointments, and ordering supplies or equipment as necessary.

Weigh envelopes containing statements to determine correct postage and affix postage, using stamps or metering equipment; sort, count, and wrap currency and coins, and perform paperwork required for monetary transactions.
Prepare bank deposits, balancing assigned funds as necessary; review compiled data on operating costs and revenues to set rates; and update manuals when rates, rules, or regulations are amended.
Monitor employee compliance with safety rules and report or address safety hazards; ensure security measures are in place and that operations comply with gaming regulations and standards.
Assist customers with queries about product locations or offers, and promote a welcoming atmosphere for patrons entering establishments; maintain confidentiality of customers' transactions.
Collect payments in various forms, issue change, and manage cash floats and cash drawers responsibly; verify signatures and required information on checks.
Calculate and record totals of transactions, ensuring all entries are accurate and properly documented; compute credit terms, discounts, or rates for goods or services to complete billing documents.
Prepare and issue tickets and other items for customers, ensuring adherence to applicable rules and regulations during service; greet customers and inquire about their service experience.
Assist customers by providing information, answering their questions, and resolving their complaints regarding accounts, loans, and services.
Monitor and maintain cash and inventory records, including counting daily cash inventories, processing transactions, and balancing cash drawers at the end of shifts.
Create and maintain records of financial transactions for customers, including accounts, loans, and payments; document customer account activity and monitor overdue accounts.
Advise clients on financial matters including debt management, budgeting, and housing options, while recommending strategies to meet their financial goals.
Process and verify transactions such as deposits, withdrawals, payments, and loans; ensure accuracy in processing customer transactions and maintaining financial records.
Analyze clients' financial situations and loan applications to determine eligibility for financial products, while explaining the terms and conditions of loans and credit options.
Plan and execute fundraising activities, monitor financial data, and develop relationships with potential donors to increase contributions and participation in events.
Conduct research and analyze financial information to provide recommendations and develop plans for clients' financial goals, as well as collecting pertinent data for loan approval processes.
Provide clerical support by sorting correspondence, filing documents, and performing various administrative functions for maintaining financial records and customer accounts.
Negotiate with creditors on behalf of clients and assist in creating debt management plans and budgets to avoid insolvency or foreclosure.

Maintain and review account records, updating and recategorizing them according to status changes. Inform individuals and groups about the financial assistance available to college or university students. Contact applicants or creditors to resolve questions about applications or assist with completion of paperwork. Contact insurance companies to check on status of claims payments and write appeal letters for denials on claims. Inform clients about payment priorities according to credit terms and interest rates to reduce overall costs. Counsel clients on personal and family financial problems, such as excessive spending or borrowing of funds. Match individuals' needs and eligibility with available financial aid programs to provide informed recommendations. Assist in the selection of financial award candidates using electronic databases to certify loan eligibility. Notify credit departments, order merchandise repossession, and turn over account records to attorneys when customers fail to respond to collection attempts. Trace delinquent customers to new addresses by inquiring at post offices or through questioning neighbors. Negotiate credit extensions when necessary. Arrange for debt repayment or establish repayment schedules based on customers' financial situations. Calculate the amount of debt and funds available to plan methods of payoff and estimate time for debt liquidation. Check figures, postings, and documents for correct entry and mathematical accuracy. Monitor status of loans and accounts to ensure that payments are up to date. Prepare and submit tax forms and returns. Compile budget data and documents based on estimated revenues and expenses and previous budgets. Prepare purchase orders and expense reports. Prepare trial balances of books. Debit, credit, and total accounts on computer spreadsheets and databases, using specialized accounting software. Reconcile records of bank transactions. Prepare documents such as work orders, bills of lading, or shipping orders. Gather and organize information on financial problems or procedures. Analyze gathered data and develop solutions or alternative methods of proceeding. Document findings and prepare recommendations for new systems or changes. Develop and implement records management program for filing and protection of records. Prepare manuals and train workers in new procedures according to organizational policy. Develop a clear understanding of customers' needs and take actions to ensure that such needs are met.

Perform financial calculations, such as amounts due, interest charges, balances, and discounts. Record shipment data, such as weight, charges, space availability, damages, or discrepancies for reporting, accounting, or recordkeeping purposes. Collect, sort, distribute, or prepare mail. Perform general office duties, such as filing, answering telephones, and handling routine correspondence. Provide information about the establishment, such as location of departments or offices, or services provided. Schedule appointments and maintain and update appointment calendars. Transmit documents to customers, using computer or mail. Support the development of training materials and technical manuals. Manage subcontractor activities, reviewing proposals and developing performance specifications. Collaborate with departments to meet customer requirements and minimize negative impacts on the business. Protect proprietary materials. Apply analytic methods to control logistics operations. Monitor logistics standards and trends to improve planning or execution. Interpret logistics elements such as supply chain management.

Manage the logistical aspects of product life cycles, including coordination or provisioning of samples, and minimization of obsolescence. Design and set up solutions for logistical support activities, including maintenance planning, repair analysis, and transportation recommendations. Analyze project plans and provide project management services, including the provision and analysis of technical data. Prepare estimates and documentation as part of the project management process. Participate in reviewing and assessing design alternatives and their impacts. Develop technical project management tools such as plans and responsibility matrices. Plan logistics support activities and execute shipping or routing of materials. Develop compliance requirements with environmental standards. Maintain positive business relationships with key personnel involved in logistics activities.

Prepare financial statements, billing information, and account statements according to established procedures. Calculate and issue bills, invoices, and total customer merchandise selections at the checkout counter. Reconcile or report discrepancies found in financial records. Calculate space available, shipping, storage, or demurrage charges. Code documents according to company procedures. Maintain inventory records and prepare purchase orders, expense reports, and payroll information. File and maintain records of accounts. Compile financial, statistical, and accounting reports relating to cash receipts, expenditures, accounts payable and receivable, and profits.

Analyze data to identify logistics improvements or to evaluate the environment or energy efficiency of logistics activities. Reorganize shipping schedules to maximize transport efficiency or minimize the environmental impact.

Perform tasks related to shipping materials including packing, sealing, labeling, or preparing for shipping. This includes gathering and organizing shipment contents, verifying accuracy against records, and making arrangements with carrier representatives for delivery.
Analyze and determine methods to enhance transportation or logistical operations, including optimizing routing and shipment processes.

Review and assess equipment and storage requirements for logistical operations. Recommend the purchase of storage equipment and design layouts for optimizing space. Determine packaging requirements based on shipping and environmental efficiencies.
Remove irrelevant tasks related to customer service in retail environments, stock management, and non-financial administrative roles.
Identify opportunities for inventory reductions.
Apply logistics modeling techniques to enhance operational processes, facility design, or layouts.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current or future logistical processes to recommend improvements.
Provide logistics technology and information to support efficient product, equipment, or service manufacturing.
Analyze logistics data related to customer service, forecasting, procurement, manufacturing, inventory, transportation, or warehousing.
Conduct various logistics analyses, such as time studies, network analyses, or flow-path analyses to support operational improvements.
Determine logistics support requirements, including facility details, staffing needs, and maintenance plans.
Assess the feasibility of designing new facilities or modifying existing ones based on cost, space, technical needs, or ergonomics.
Develop and maintain cost estimates, forecasts, or cost models for logistics operations.
Identify or establish business rules or standard operating procedures to streamline logistics processes.
Review contractual commitments and customer specifications to determine logistics requirements.
Develop specifications for equipment, tools, facility layouts, and material-handling systems.
Design effective plant distribution centers.
Oversee the work of logistics analysts to ensure project success.
Develop and maintain payment systems for accuracy in vendor payments.
Create and maintain freight rate databases for transportation cost determinations in supply chain management.
Propose logistics solutions that effectively meet customer needs.
Contact potential vendors to assess material availability for operations.
Develop models for logistics applications, including demand forecasting and cost estimating.
Ensure effective maintenance of logistics records per corporate policies.
Maintain databases of logistics-related information.
Monitor inventory transactions in warehouses to evaluate efficiency.
Manage pricing structures to reflect logistics costing accurately.
Prepare detailed reports on logistics performance metrics.
Conduct ongoing analyses regarding transportation costs and delivery processes to identify cost-saving opportunities.
Review logistics operations against performance targets and customer agreements.
Develop documentation for logistics processes to ensure compliance and efficiency.
Track the flow of products from origin to final delivery to ensure timely and accurate distribution.
Maintain communication with service providers to monitor logistics operations.
Request rates or schedules from carriers to inform cost and efficiency decisions.
Create predictive models to analyze the impact of changing logistics factors, such as fuel pricing and regulations.
Interview staff or survey facilities to identify opportunities for process improvement and cost reduction.
Conduct environmental audits for logistical activities to assess sustainability impacts.
Design comprehensive supply chain strategies to minimize environmental impacts and costs.
Prepare logistic strategy and conceptual designs for production facilities to optimize operations.
Evaluate technologies to improve transportation efficiency, including GPS and RFID systems.
Organize and manage detailed records for office or warehouse logistics through data entry and retrieval.
Gather and input information from customers for account setup and service applications.
Respond to customer inquiries and complaints effectively, maintaining a high level of customer service.
Prepare and review financial documents, such as claims and reports, ensuring completeness and compliance.
Verify the accuracy of customer accounts to meet financial regulatory standards.
Check inventory records to ensure product availability for customer requests.
Check and confirm order information for correctness to maintain customer satisfaction.
Notify departments of low supply levels to ensure timely replenishment.
Inspect logistical outputs to comply with customer specifications and quality standards.
Review orders for compliance with reporting procedures and initiate further processing when necessary.
Calculate total charges for products and services to ensure accurate billing.
Evaluate customers and their needs to recommend suitable products or services.

Read incoming correspondence to ascertain the nature of writers' concerns, determine the disposition of correspondence, and route it to appropriate departments for reply. Compose letters in response to requests concerning merchandise, damage claims, credit information, delinquent accounts, incorrect billing, or unsatisfactory service. Gather records pertinent to specific problems, review them for completeness and accuracy, and prepare associated documents such as acknowledgment letters, damage claims, and invoices. Compile data from records to prepare periodic reports and ensure that money collected is properly recorded and secured.

Complete form letters in response to requests or problems identified by correspondence. Review correspondence for format and typographical accuracy, assemble the information into a prescribed form, and submit it for signature. Process orders for goods requested in correspondence and confer with company personnel regarding the feasibility of complying with writers' requests. Compute costs of records furnished to requesters, and write letters to obtain payment. Prepare records for shipment by certified mail and present clear explanations of governing rules and regulations.
